# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class MarshaledCookieStore < ActiveSupport::Cache::Store
  def initialize(options={})
    super(options)
  end

  def read(key, options = nil)
    if serialized_cookie_data = cookies[key.to_s]
      Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(serialized_cookie_data))
    end
  end

  def write(key, value, options = nil)
    serialized_cookie_data = Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(value))
    cookies[key.to_s] = serialized_cookie_data
  end

  def delete(key, options = nil)
    cookies.delete(key.to_s)
  end

  private

  def cookies
    Thread.current[:action_dispatch_cookies] ||= {}
  end
end

# To use the above session store, the following line needs to be added in config/initializers/session_store.rb
# Rails.application.config.session_store :marshaled_cookie_store
